'''John Stankevitch''' (born 6 November 1979 in [[Whiston, Merseyside]], England)<ref>{{cite web
|url=http://www.saints.org.uk/home/player.php?num=16367
|title=John Stankevitch
|website=St Helens RLFC
|accessdate=21 March 2016 }}</ref> is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s, and [[Coach (sport)|coach]]ed in the 2000s and 2010s. Stankevitch played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] and the [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] in the [[Super League]] as a {{rlp|sr}} and as a {{rlp|pr}}.

==Playing career==
Having won the 1999 Championship, [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] contested the [[2000 World Club Challenge]] against [[National Rugby League]] Premiers the [[Melbourne Storm]], with Stankevitch playing from the interchange bench in the loss. This was Stankevitch's First Team début.{{fact|date=March 2022}} 

As [[Super League V]] champions, [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] played against [[2000 NRL season|2000 NRL]] Premiers, the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[2001 World Club Challenge]]. Stankevitch played from the interchange bench in Saints' victory. Stankevitch played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] from the interchange bench in their [[2002 Super League Grand Final]] victory against the [[Bradford Bulls]]. Having won [[Super League VI]], St Helens contested the [[2003 World Club Challenge]] against 2002 NRL Premiers, the [[Sydney Roosters]]. Stankevitch played at {{rlp|pr}} in Saints' 38-0 loss.{{fact|date=March 2022}} 

==Coaching career==
Since retiring from playing, through a serious shoulder and neck injury in 2005, Stankevitch has coached at numerous Rugby League and Rugby Union clubs including stints at [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] and [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]].{{fact|date=March 2022}}

===Widnes Vikings===
Beginning his coaching career at his home town club Widnes Vikings, as Senior Academy Head Coach in 2006, Stankevitch led the side to 2 Grand Finals in two seasons, and assisted in the development of players such as Catalans Dragons' Richie Myler, Bradford Bulls star Adam Sidlow, Leeds Rhinos {{rlp|pr}} Anthony Mullally and current Man Of Steel, Castleford Tigers star man Luke Gale.{{fact|date=March 2022}}

===Doncaster RLFC===
In 2007 Stankevitch was approached by Championship side Doncaster RL and asked to take over as head coach, midway through the season. It was a tough ask, as Doncaster sat bottom of the table, 13 points adrift of safety with only 7 league games remaining. Despite taking over and building a team from scratch inside a week (players had been released due to club administration), the team were relegated at the end of the 2007 season, despite winning 4 of their last 7 games.{{fact|date=March 2022}}
===Widnes Vikings (return)===
At the end of the 2007 season Stankevitch tendered his resignation in order to return to the Widnes Vikings as assistant coach, working with the First Team squad on a full-time basis and he held that role until January 2009 when he was appointed caretaker head coach, after the dismissal of [[Steve McCormack]].{{fact|date=March 2022}}

Stankevitch, in the games that followed, led the Vikings to a record [[Challenge Cup]] victory against [[Saddleworth]] Rangers. He also led the first team to a televised victory against [[Toulouse Olympique]] in which the team won by 72-0, in Round 1 of the League season. Stankevitch also led the team through the group stages of the [[Northern Rail Cup]], and the club went on to win the Cup later in that year.{{fact|date=March 2022}}

Stankevitch left the club in June 2009, after the appointment of [[Paul Cullen (rugby league)|Paul Cullen]] as head coach and the club reverting to part-time.{{fact|date=March 2022}}
===Rotherham Titans RU===
Stankevitch was recruited as backs coach and coaching advisor at Rugby Union Championship side [[Rotherham Titans]], where he spent 6 months.{{fact|date=March 2022}}
===Rochdale Hornets===
Stankevitch joined [[Rochdale Hornets]] in Championship 1 ahead of the 2010 season, in the position of head coach. He led the Rochdale Hornets club to Play Off finishing positions in 2010, 2011 and 2012.{{fact|date=March 2022}}
In November 2012, Stankevitch left his role as head coach at Rochdale Hornets and he is yet to return to Professional coaching as of 2022.{{fact|date=March 2022}}

===Russia===
In October 2011, Stankevitch was appointed as Performance Coaching Consultant with [[Russia national rugby league team|Russia]] for their [[2013 Rugby League World Cup qualifying|World Cup Qualifying fixtures]] and acted as head coach for the games against [[Italy national rugby league team|Italy]], [[Lebanon national rugby league team|Lebanon]] and [[Serbia national rugby league team|Serbia]].{{fact|date=March 2022}}
